spec_match.rationale: The mental model (partitioned bytes vs shared bytes per library) is right, but the example libraries are wrong — chromestatus explicitly excludes self-hosted-by-version libraries (React, Vue, jQuery, Lodash) because their versioning is a manual embedder decision. The calculator preset essentially over-counts savings on resources that wouldn't actually qualify.

interactive.rationale: Editable library list, traffic sliders, cache-hit-rate sliders, calculate button, and a per-library before/after bar chart — real interactive surface well past static-card.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises editable library sizes, visitor/pages inputs, partitioned-vs-shared sliders, byte/CO2/percentage outputs, and per-library bar charts — the page delivers all of those.

edge_cases.rationale: Cold-cache new-visitor path and repeat-page same-origin hit are modelled, but no handling of the eligibility-versus-not split (selected libraries are all-or-nothing pervasive), no SRI mismatch, no cache eviction churn, and CO2 figure (0.6 g/GB) has no provenance.

references.rationale: The page has no see-also or references section at all — the chromestatus link and feature page link are entirely missing, breaking the self-heal invariant in CLAUDE.md #3.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables and the result/savings cards have legible contrast, but `.calc-btn` overrides `background: var(--text-black)` and `color: var(--bg-ivory)` together (good) — however the inline `accent-color: var(--accent-blue)` on checkboxes is fine. Main miss: the white text on `--accent-emerald` value cards risks failing AA at 1.3rem (large-text 3:1 threshold may pass but normal-text 4.5:1 fails).

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus link and see-also block
openQuestions.detail: The page is missing the mandatory `chromestatus.com/feature/<id>` link that the self-heal script in CLAUDE.md depends on. Add a see-also section identical to the other concepts in this folder.

openQuestions.title: Correct the eligibility model: presets do not all qualify
openQuestions.detail: Chromestatus explicitly excludes self-hosted-by-version libraries (React/Vue/jQuery/Lodash/Tailwind via the npm-pinned URL) and includes analytics scripts, social embeds, video players, captcha, and ads libraries. Swap the preset list to those actual qualifying categories — or split the table into 'eligible' and 'common-but-not-eligible' rows so the calculation reflects real allowlist behaviour.

openQuestions.title: Cite the CO2 conversion factor
openQuestions.detail: The page emits a CO2-saved figure based on 0.6 g/GB with no source. Either cite the Sustainable Web Manifesto / Website Carbon constant being used, or replace it with a transparent slider so the visitor controls the assumption.

openQuestions.title: Show a 'first request fills the bucket' breakdown
openQuestions.detail: The current model assumes a steady-state hit rate. Spec behaviour is that the very first user across the entire web who fetches an allowlisted URL still pays a network cost. Add a small annotation, or a separate 'first 24 hours after a new allowlist entry' mode, so visitors don't over-attribute steady-state savings to day-one.

summary: Real, ambitious interactive calculator — strongest interactive surface in this feature folder — but it models the wrong libraries (chromestatus excludes self-hosted-by-version frameworks) and ships without the mandatory chromestatus link. Fix the eligible-libraries list, add the references block, and source the CO2 constant.
